prompt: Write the opening paragraph of a children's book about a shy octopus who wants to make friends.
response: Deep beneath the shimmering waves, in a cozy nook of coral and seashells, lived a little octopus named Ollie. Ollie had eight soft, wiggly arms, eyes that sparkled like polished pearls, and a heart full of kindness—but he was terribly, wonderfully shy. Every morning, he’d peek out from behind his favorite anemone and watch the other sea creatures playing tag with bubbles, building sandcastles on the ocean floor, or singing songs with the dolphins. Ollie longed to join them, to share his favorite game of hide-and-seek (he was *very* good at it!), but whenever he tried to swim closer, his arms would tangle with nerves, and he’d dart back to his quiet corner. “Maybe tomorrow,” he’d whisper to a passing hermit crab. But deep down, Ollie knew—he really, truly wanted a friend.
